issue.title: Classify profile adapters for core promotion

policy.rule: Every current WordPress-profile method adapter and compiler script adapter must have an explicit classification, rationale, follow-up owner, and promotion gate before adoption CI can remain green.
policy.oracle_condition: Keep moving repeated generic constructs out of WphxPhpWordPressAdapters.hx and into reusable core IR; make profile growth trend-gated, not merely pass/fail gated.

adapter_classifications.rationale: These bodies combine WordPress ABI calls with reusable PHP mechanics: callable arrays, dynamic helper calls, hook payload arrays, nested mutation, and callback-style control flow.
adapter_classifications.promotion_gate: Before expanding callback-heavy HTTP/embed adapters, add a core callable/closure fixture that covers callable arrays, call_user_func-style dispatch, accepted-args behavior, and reference-sensitive payload mutation.

adapter_classifications.rationale: These bodies combine WordPress ABI calls with reusable PHP mechanics: callable arrays, dynamic helper calls, hook payload arrays, nested mutation, and callback-style control flow.
adapter_classifications.promotion_gate: Before expanding callback-heavy HTTP/embed adapters, add a core callable/closure fixture that covers callable arrays, call_user_func-style dispatch, accepted-args behavior, and reference-sensitive payload mutation.

adapter_classifications.rationale: These adapters are larger orchestration bodies. If similar slices keep appearing, Adapter IR will be doing backend work rather than bounded public ABI shaping.
adapter_classifications.promotion_gate: Before adding comparable orchestration adapters, either reduce the repeated constructs into reusable core IR or file backend-promotion evidence through the private-emitter ladder.

adapter_classifications.rationale: These WP_Embed methods are profile-sensitive because their value is hook timing, shortcode registration, cache-post timing, and filter-visible WordPress output behavior.
adapter_classifications.promotion_gate: Hold these as profile-only until the embed whole-file inventory separates hook/shortcode ABI from generic callback, array, and output-buffer lowering needs.

adapter_classifications.rationale: These are WordPress HTTP policy and URL-safety boundaries whose observable value is matching core constants, filters, and public method ABI, even though individual expressions may later use core IR.
adapter_classifications.promotion_gate: Keep these classified as profile ABI constraints until whole-file HTTP inventory shows repeated generic lowering pressure that is not specific to WordPress HTTP policy.

adapter_classifications.rationale: These are WordPress HTTP policy and URL-safety boundaries whose observable value is matching core constants, filters, and public method ABI, even though individual expressions may later use core IR.
adapter_classifications.promotion_gate: Keep these classified as profile ABI constraints until whole-file HTTP inventory shows repeated generic lowering pressure that is not specific to WordPress HTTP policy.

adapter_classifications.rationale: These adapters repeatedly exercise native associative arrays, object-field storage, falsey-value-preserving reads, array appends, and structured header/cookie mutation that should be reusable PHP core IR.
adapter_classifications.promotion_gate: Before adding similar header, response, or cookie profile bodies, promote the repeated array read/write/append/isset/empty/null distinction behavior into a generic native-array core fixture.

adapter_classifications.rationale: These wrappers are dominated by WordPress-visible proxy/encoding constants, method names, and PHP extension handoff policy; broad behavior remains a file/domain ownership question.
adapter_classifications.promotion_gate: Do not broaden beyond selected adapters until HTTP whole-file ownership gates inventory proxy, TLS, headers, streams, live transport, and Requests handoff behavior.

adapter_classifications.rationale: The early-provider adapters exist because WP_oEmbed exposes static provider queues and mutable native arrays; the useful compiler pressure is static-property and dynamic-member lowering.
adapter_classifications.promotion_gate: Before claiming broader WP_oEmbed ownership, move static property defaults, static array mutation, dynamic class/member access, and class-string behavior into reusable core IR fixtures.

adapter_classifications.rationale: The early-provider adapters exist because WP_oEmbed exposes static provider queues and mutable native arrays; the useful compiler pressure is static-property and dynamic-member lowering.
adapter_classifications.promotion_gate: Before claiming broader WP_oEmbed ownership, move static property defaults, static array mutation, dynamic class/member access, and class-string behavior into reusable core IR fixtures.

adapter_classifications.rationale: The admin HXX pilot is a Haxe-owned typed markup unit, but it still enters WPHX PHP through a named file-segment adapter. The reusable compiler pressure is typed markup AST/input metadata lowering into a generic segment plan.
adapter_classifications.promotion_gate: Before adding additional HXX-owned admin/template units, promote the typed AST helper path into a reusable HXX/file-segment lowering API with source-position diagnostics and invalid-template checks.

adapter_classifications.rationale: These named script switches represent generic file-scope statement emission, require/require_once, include timing, and return/direct-script behavior.
adapter_classifications.promotion_gate: Move direct file-scope statements and include/require emission toward a reusable file-segment IR API before adding more named script switches.

adapter_classifications.rationale: These named script switches represent generic file-scope statement emission, require/require_once, include timing, and return/direct-script behavior.
adapter_classifications.promotion_gate: Move direct file-scope statements and include/require emission toward a reusable file-segment IR API before adding more named script switches.

adapter_classifications.rationale: The template-segment switches are minimized mixed-template probes; keeping them named is acceptable only while the segment model is still fixture-bounded.
adapter_classifications.promotion_gate: Replace named template script switches with generic segment registration and caller-scope metadata before claiming mixed-template ownership.

adapter_classifications.rationale: The template-segment switches are minimized mixed-template probes; keeping them named is acceptable only while the segment model is still fixture-bounded.
adapter_classifications.promotion_gate: Replace named template script switches with generic segment registration and caller-scope metadata before claiming mixed-template ownership.

adapter_classifications.rationale: The template-segment switches are minimized mixed-template probes; keeping them named is acceptable only while the segment model is still fixture-bounded.
adapter_classifications.promotion_gate: Replace named template script switches with generic segment registration and caller-scope metadata before claiming mixed-template ownership.

adapter_classifications.rationale: The default theme HXX pilot is a Haxe-owned typed markup unit, but it still enters WPHX PHP through a named file-segment adapter. The reusable compiler pressure is typed theme markup AST/input metadata lowering into a generic segment plan.
adapter_classifications.promotion_gate: Before adding additional HXX-owned theme/template units, promote the typed AST helper path into a reusable HXX/file-segment lowering API with source-position diagnostics, invalid-template checks, theme template-loader context metadata, and browser/visual evidence gates.

claims: All current WPHX PHP WordPress-profile method adapters and compiler script adapters are explicitly classified for profile/core promotion pressure.
The audit is executable and fails when adapter registries drift without updating classification, rationale, owner, and promotion gate.
The audit records which current adapters are profile-only ABI constraints, reusable core IR candidates, temporary bridges, or backend-promotion pressure.
non_claims: This audit does not move any adapter out of the WordPress profile by itself.
This audit does not claim WPHX PHP is a mature arbitrary-Haxe PHP backend.
This audit does not claim stock Haxe PHP can be abandoned as private implementation emitter.
This audit does not claim full class-wp-http.php, class-wp-embed.php, or class-wp-oembed.php ownership.
